content.text:
All green. The launcher relaunched clean:

- **`launcher.exe` pid 33482, 1.5% CPU** вҖ” idle and healthy, *not* spinning (contrast the 99% orphan we just killed)
- **Fresh auth token received** `00:56:54` вҖ” this is the durable one, unlike the short-lived direct-launch token that dropped you before
- **Window up:** `Homecoming: City of Heroes`
- `live:0.7.1` profile and `hc_live` package both confirmed up to date вҖ” no re-download

**Your move:** the Homecoming launcher window is now frontmost вҖ” **click Play**. Because this session has a freshly-minted token (not the borrowed one-shot token from my direct launch), it should hold the connection through character select and into a zone and *stay* there.

`вҳ… Insight в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`
- The difference between my two launches is a real lesson in **auth token lifecycle**: a token minted for a specific action expires fast (you loaded a map, then got dropped). Letting `launcher.exe` request its own token at Play-time gives a session-scoped token tied to the live client's heartbeat вҖ” that's what makes it durable.
- `launcher.exe` sitting at ~1.5% CPU is the correct idle signature. If you ever see *any* Wine process pinned at ~100% with no visible game window, that's the orphaned-`explorer.exe` pattern again вҖ” `wineserver -k` is the clean reset.
`в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ`

## Final resolved state

| Item | Status |
|------|--------|
| Orphaned `explorer.exe` @ 99% CPU | вң… Killed |
| `nohwaccel` | вң… `false` (HW accel on) |
| Broken `dosdevices` d:/e: | вң… Removed (only c: + z:) |
| `launch.sh` double slash | вң… Fixed + executable |
| `process.json` stale PID | вң… Reset to `{"live":[]}` |
| Fresh launcher session | вң… Up, authenticated, window ready |
| Full stack proven | вң… Already loaded into a live zone once |

Everything I can resolve from the shell is done. The one thing only you can do is **click Play** вҖ” tell me if the game holds the session this time or drops again, and if it drops I'll pull the launcher's own game-launch log (not my `/tmp` capture) to see the exact disconnect reason.
